title: Berry Connection
summary: The Berry connection is the U(1) connection on the line bundle of occupied states over regular momentum space, encoding phase transport of eigenstates along paths.
assumptions: The occupied state is isolated from the empty state over the region of interest.
A smooth local phase choice exists away from singularities.
regime: Adiabatic geometry of a gapped band subspace away from bad points.
scope: Lecture One U(1) Berry connection for a two-band problem with one filled state.

source_anchors.notes: Parallel transport of phase is the defining geometric picture.

symmetries: U(1) gauge redundancy in phase choice.
representation: Connection one-form on the occupied-state bundle.
failure_modes: At bad points the bundle is singular and the U(1) connection is not globally defined.
retrieval_hints: Use for Berry phase, holonomy, or line-bundle questions.
